channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzanian Lawyers Highlight Legal Challenges in Prison Consultations for Tundu Lissu
item.description: Lawyers representing Tundu Lissu, the Chadema national chairman, have testified in court about restricted access to legal consultations while he is imprisoned at Ukonga Prison. The witnesses, Nashon N...

item.title: PML-N proposes constitutional change to appoint Nawaz Sharif as AJK chief minister
item.description: The Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari has alleged that the Pakistan Muslim League-Nawaz (PML-N) is pushing for a constitutional amendment to allow three-time former Prime Mi...

item.title: Chery Q Electric Hatchback Set to Launch in South Africa at R350k
item.description: Chery SA has confirmed the arrival of its new Q electric hatchback in South African showrooms by early September 2026. Positioned as the brand's most affordable new energy vehicle in the country, the ...

item.title: Israel's Land Sales in the West Bank to Jewish Americans Spark Controversy
item.description: The article details how Israeli real estate companies are facilitating the sale of land in the West Bank to Jewish American citizens, a practice critics argue exacerbates the Israeli-Palestinian confl...

item.title: Personal reflections on media nostalgia
item.description: The article compiles a series of nostalgic anecdotes from readers about their childhood experiences with television programs and technological changes. Paul Taylor jokes about the BYD car badge appear...

item.title: South Africa's Inflation Surpasses 5% in June, Prompting Possible Rate Hike
item.description: Consumer inflation in South Africa accelerated to 5% year-on-year in June, up from 4.5% in May, according to data released on Wednesday. This surge, driven primarily by transport costs (12.7%) and hou...

item.title: Political Pressure Shapes Finalization of Gordie Howe Bridge Agreement
item.description: The Canadian government has released detailed information about the Gordie Howe Bridge agreement, which faces significant political pressure from both Canada and the United States. The deal, aimed at ...

item.title: Matiangi Challenges Sifuna to Mobilize Western Kenya Support, Claims Kisii is Secure
item.description: Jubilee Party Deputy Leader Fred Matiang'i met Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna and Linda Mwananchi's team at his Karen office, challenging Sifuna to consolidate opposition support in Western Kenya. Matia...

item.title: Tanzania's Sports and Business Updates: New Players, Coaches, and Maritime Expansion
item.description: The article highlights recent developments in Tanzania's sports and business sectors. Yanga Football Club has welcomed new winger Abushiri, ready to contribute to the team's challenges. The club has a...

item.title: Origin Energy cybersecurity incident exposes millions of customer records
item.description: Origin Energy, Australia's largest energy provider, confirmed a cybersecurity incident where unauthorised access to customer data may have occurred. The breach potentially exposed personal information...

item.title: Media Group Condemns Attempted Arrest of Photojournalist Covering Pasay Protest
item.description: A photojournalist covering a protest in Pasay City alleged that police officers attempted to arrest and handcuff him while he was documenting the demonstration. Bullit Marquez said the incident happen...

item.title: Federal High Court Adjourns Trial of Five Men Accused of Hiding Former Governor's Location
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja adjourned the trial of five men accused of hiding the whereabouts of former Bayelsa State Governor Timipre Sylva until October 5. The trial, which was set to begin befo...

item.title: Hackney council to demolish beloved football pitch for social housing, sparking community concern
item.description: Hackney council plans to demolish the Keith Miller sports pitch, a beloved football cage in east London, to construct urgently needed social homes. The site, known locally as the 'San Siro of east Lon...

item.title: Vancouver Judge Sentences Man for Attempting to Smuggle Drugs into Canada
item.description: A Vancouver judge has sentenced a man described as a 'mad man' for planning to smuggle large quantities of drugs into Canada using a jet ski. The case highlights the lengths some individuals will go t...

item.title: French Modelling Scout Linked to Epstein Found Dead Amid Allegations of Abuse
item.description: A French modelling scout, Daniel Siad, 69, with close ties to convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home outside Paris. Prosecutors and his lawyer confirmed the death, notin...

item.title: Managing Living Costs Through Budgeting and Financial Discipline
item.description: The article discusses strategies for Tanzanians to manage rising living costs by adopting budgeting practices. Financial experts emphasize the importance of creating a budget based on income to ensure...

item.title: President Marcos Allocates P3.4 Billion for BARMM Peace and Development Projects
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has allocated P3.4 billion to advance peace and development initiatives in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region of Muslim Mindanao (BARMM). The funds aim to address longstan...

item.title: Arsenal Signs Spanish Goalkeeper Misa Rodriguez on Free Transfer
item.description: Arsenal Women's team has officially signed Spanish goalkeeper Misa Rodriguez on a free transfer, marking her sixth summer addition to the squad. The 27-year-old, who previously played for Real Madrid,...

item.title: Northern Cape man jailed for touching stepdaughter's buttocks
item.description: A Northern Cape man, Monapule Rantsi, has been sentenced to five years in prison for sexually assaulting his girlfriend’s teenage daughter. The incident occurred on December 11, 2025, in Homestead, Ki...

item.title: NSUI Calls for CBI and SFIO Probe into ITPI Recognition Dispute
item.description: The National Students' Union of India (NSUI) has demanded a thorough investigation by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) and Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO) into the recognition proce...
